input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade the .testsettings seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

data.content:

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** in MSTest v3. You need to replace it with a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent migration for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <!-- MSTest-specific settings -->
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution/TestDirectory deployment behavior -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

</RunSettings>

data.content: Now reference it in your project:

Here's what changed and why:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` in `<RunConfiguration>` |
| `<AssemblyResolution>` / `<TestDirectory>` | `<DeploymentEnabled>true</DeploymentEnabled>` in `<MSTest>` |
| `<DataCollector>` (code coverage v1.0) | `<DataCollector>` under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` (v2.0 URI) |

**Key notes:**
- The old `.testsettings` file can be deleted — MSTest v3 ignores it entirely.
- For **per-test** timeouts (instead of session-wide), use `[Timeout(30000)]` on test methods or set `<TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` inside the `<MSTest>` node.
- Run with: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings` (or rely on the `<RunSettingsFilePath>` property added to the `.csproj`).
